stem: Compute the modulus \( \left| ([[a]]) + ([[b]])i \right| \) of the complex number \( z = ([[a]]) + ([[b]])i \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the absolute values of the parts; the modulus uses the Pythagorean combination \( \sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}} \).
This is \( a^{2}+b^{2} \), the square of the modulus; the square root is still needed.
This takes the square root of the sum of the parts instead of the sum of their squares.
This is off by one from the correct modulus.
This is off by one from the correct modulus.
This doubles the modulus.

stem: Find the principal argument \( \arg(z) \) (in radians, in the range \( (-\pi, \pi] \)) of \( z = ([[a]]) + ([[b]])i \).
solution: Since \( z = ([[a]]) + ([[b]])i \) lies along a standard direction, \( \arg(z) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign; the argument was reflected across the real axis.
This reflects the angle across the imaginary axis instead of reading it directly.
This adds an extra quarter turn to the correct angle.
This subtracts an extra quarter turn from the correct angle.
This adds an extra half turn to the correct angle.
This doubles the correct angle.

stem: A complex number is given in polar form as \( z = [[r]]\left(\cos\dfrac{\pi [[k]]}{6} + i\sin\dfrac{\pi [[k]]}{6}\right) \). Write \( z \) in rectangular form \( x + yi \).
distractor_rationales: This swaps sine and cosine; the real part must be \( r\cos\theta \).
This is the conjugate; the sign of the imaginary part was flipped.
This forgets to scale by the modulus \( r \).
This negates the correct value (a sign slip).
This is off by one in the real part.
This is off by one in the imaginary part.
This adds the two moduli instead of multiplying them.
This multiplies \( |z_1|^{2} \) and \( |z_2|^{2} \) (forgetting the square roots), giving the square of the correct answer for generic inputs.
This is the modulus of the sum \( z_1+z_2 \), not the product \( z_1 z_2 \).
This negates the correct value (a sign slip); a modulus cannot be negative.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
